channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: On hansel road, behind a small building unit (electrical building), 30th May
item.description:
A lot of waste left there, this keeps happening in this location. There is a mattress, a hoover and several other items.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hansel Road, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load

item.title: 8 Feet tall grass weeds, 30th May
item.description:
The grass weeds in this alleyway in Glenister Road (just off Glenforth Street) has grown into the gardens. Can you kindly help. Please note, this was reported on the 21st of April ref 9329689 and updated on the 2nd of May. Then was raised again on the 10th of May ref 9428781. Reports fairly quickly marked as fixed “this issue will be resolved during next week, scheduled, weekly cleanse”. To make things worse, the entrance to the alleyway has now become a fly tipping area.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Caletock Way, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Missing street cleaner! 🤔, 30th May
item.description:
Can I report our street cleaner as a missing person? He has not been seen by anyone at the junction of Weir Hall Road and Weir Hall Avenue N18 for at least 2 weeks, possibly 3. We know he spends more time on his phone than actually cleaning but anything is better than nothing......especially as we have reached that time of year that your parks department feel the need to mow the common grass areas every 20 minutes and they just go straight over any litter making 5 pieces into 500,000 pieces(never happened years ago, the guy used to pick it up before mowing.....I suppose this is progress!). There is alot of broken glass in the road by the roundabout in Weir Hall Avenue on both sides, going up to about nos 61. Also, in the stretch of Weir Hall Avenue that has no houses that leads to the cul de sac there is getting quite a build up of litter where the dealers park and also opposite there on the common grass area people are dumping stuff again. In Weir Hall Road its building up again, general litter and also a plastic crate appeared this morning outside number 60. When are the cleaners coming back?
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Footpath From Weir Hall Road To 61-63 Weir Hall Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No rubbish collection, streets are filthy, 30th May
item.description:
No one had collected the rubbish as scheduled. Now the foxes have torn open all the bins and food waste. Come on Bromley, this is basic stuff. Sort it out

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maitland Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Playground in disrepair, 30th May
item.description:
The playground has fallen into a state of disrepair and presents a serious risk of injury to children and other members of the public. Issues include broken equipment, damaged flooring, exposed metal, loose bolts, broken fencing, sharp edges, etc. Children cannot use it. Needs fixing

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sibthorpe Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Kempe Road, Queen's park on the corner of Milman Road, 30th May
item.description:
Just appeared over night

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Milman Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Rubbish overfloading, 30th May
item.description:
This is an ongoing problem (recurring fortnightly). These people are not doing recycling and therefore their non-recycling bin are exploding, since they are collected every 2 weeks. This is a very unsocial behavior that leaves the street smelling and look not looked after.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 13, Waldegrave Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Many potholes across this stretch of road, 30th May
item.description:
It has been there for at least 6 months
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Molescroft, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 30th May
item.description:
There's a dead fox laid sideways alongside the path in the open grass. You can ot avoid it if you walk in the path. I first saw it on 29 May 2026. It must be unpleasant for parents with their children and dog walkers. I'm not sharing a photo as it is unpleasant.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rangefield Road, Downham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 30th May
item.description:
Fly tipping! Constant, constant fly tipping!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tremaine Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Van pulled up emptied rubbish in drint of recycling bins, 30th May
item.description:
More people driving in to estate and dumping rubbish causing rats
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oldfield Grove, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Not a storage cupboard, 30th May
item.description:
The disabled toilet by the cafe is being used as a storage cupboard for cleaning supplies. Wheelchair users such as myself need space to be able to use the toilet and having brooms and mops by the toilet hinders us. I didn't look at the chemicals I there but I don't think that is safe either.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stone Park Avenue, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Inflatable, 30th May
item.description:
Inflatable

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rochford Walk, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Useless Forest blocking camberwell Church Street again, 30th May
item.description:
Day 456. Remove it

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 63, Camberwell Church Street, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Nameplate illegible, 30th May
item.description:
Please change for new.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 126, East Dulwich Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shutter on side of pub vandalism, 30th May
item.description:
Please clean.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 17, Spurling Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Crappy Flag, 30th May
item.description:
Several unwanted flags are in the area Now that “Raise the Colours” has been properly ummasked for the racist, xenophobic campaign we all knew it was the council needs to change their stance & actively remove them. This streetlight is now rocking at its base just to the action of weather exacerbated by the stupid unwanted flag You could of course bill the culprits who did this to Bexley They actively promoted themselves on social media and are not hard to find They also aggressively targetted Bexley & Highways agency staff who removed the flags from Roundabouts Fortunately we haven’t seen a death as has now happened in Lichfield
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 70, Hurst Road, Northumberland Heath, Erith, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins causing obstruction on pavement, 30th May
item.description:
The house appears to be unoccupied and the bins are left on the street each time the waste collection takes place as there is no one to move the bins off the pavement.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tennyson Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bin, 30th May
item.description:
Overflowing bin

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Westmount Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The usual..., 30th May
item.description:
The usual... Also distributed on pavement outside 3houses along Greenvale Road

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Westmount Road, Eltham
